decision_scope: first deterministic producer and production-reader structural core only

macos.c_libfuzzer_blocker: Apple Command Line Tools did not contain libclang_rt.fuzzer_osx.a

rust.memory_boundary: safe language subset with unsafe code forbidden
rust.checked_arithmetic: checked_add and checked_mul return explicit absence on overflow
strengths: compile-time ownership and bounds safety without garbage collection
smaller prototype source surface for the same current vector slice
explicit checked arithmetic and easy unsafe-code prohibition
costs: larger optimized binary in the measured macOS build
requires a separately provisioned Rust toolchain
compiler diversity is not parser implementation diversity

strengths: direct sanitizer and coverage-guided fuzzing instrumentation
costs: memory safety and lifetime correctness are not guaranteed by the language
more source was required for the same current vector slice
safety depends on warnings, sanitizers, fuzzing and manual review remaining enabled

rust_policy: forbid unsafe code in the first vertical slice
use no third-party crates until a consumer and evidence justify one
use explicit checked arithmetic in every untrusted structural calculation
pin the exact toolchain and commit Cargo.lock
enable overflow checks in release builds
decision.c_prototype: retain only as a non-production differential oracle and fuzz target
decision.inspect: must use an independently written parser and error path; final implementation language remains subject to the independence audit

limitations: Both prototypes implement only the current END-P0 structural vector slice.
Same-host repeated binary equality is not cross-platform reproducibility.
Ten thousand fuzz iterations are bounded evidence, not proof of defect absence.
Binary size and source lines are descriptive measurements, not standalone selection scores.
